missing_or_mismatched_facts: MISMATCH (content vs path-level impression): the plan's observed_evidence states 'Glob found NO dedicated onboarding/first-use/operator customer-entry doc', but content inspection shows a real customer-entry surface DOES exist — support/operator/first_use.py renders the customer FIRST_USE.md, support/checkers/check_first_use_wizard.py pins it, and status/kernel/gap2-customer-entry-readiness-0624.md (which the plan saw only as 'a prior work doc exists') actually documents the full entry chain (install.sh -> brick console -> cli _cmd_init -> FIRST_USE.md -> brick verify). The plan's own not_proven flags that Grep was unavailable to confirm this, so the mismatch traces to the read-only session limit, not to a scoping error.

MISMATCH (Slack): plan reported 'only brick/templates/tasks/slack-alert-all-presets-task.md found; no Slack env/config entry doc'; grep shows Slack guidance content exists in project/brick-protocol/status/kernel/install-ux-design-0618.md and the 0624 kernel doc. Again attributable to the missing Grep capability.

DELIVERABLE NOTE (not a plan-shape miss): the inline task's headline deliverable — a populated customer-entry readiness matrix (first-read / active-worktree-vs-frozen / launch route / Slack+evidence-root) — was NOT produced as filled cells. This is consistent with a plan node whose declared return_shape excludes the matrix; the plan instead bounded scope and proposed a read-enabled follow-on. The matrix itself remains for a downstream/follow-on Building.
UNADDRESSED CELL: neither the plan nor this review located/confirmed an active-worktree-vs-frozen-repo discriminator surface; the plan listed it as missing_scope and it remains unconfirmed.
narrowly_proven: The plan node's return satisfies its declared 8-field return shape and carries no forbidden/reserved keys or success/quality/Movement claims.
